title: It'S Not My Fault
lyrics:
[opening]
*clink* *splat*
Mom, don’t look at me like that—
I was just standing here with my snack!
The cup jumped right out of my hand—*whoa*—
like it wanted to go for a swim, I know!

[verse]
I didn’t even touch the table yet!
The milk was already doing its own wet!
The floor was thirsty, it needed a drink!
How was I supposed to know it would sink?

[chorus]
It’s not my fault, it’s not my fault!
The cup was acting like a vault!
It rolled and it tipped and it took a dive—
It’s not my fault it’s still alive!

*Mom sighs* "Just grab a towel."
I’m telling you, Mom, I saw it scowl!
It had a face and it looked so mean—
Like it planned the whole milk machine!

[verse]
What if the cup was a secret spy?
Sent to make the floor all sly!
It could’ve been a milk volcano—
Erupting right under my toe!

[chorus]
It’s not my fault, it’s not my fault!
The cup was acting like a vault!
It rolled and it tipped and it took a dive—
It’s not my fault it’s still alive!

*Mom laughs* "You’re ridiculous."
Okay, fine, maybe I nudged it—*just a bit*!
But the cup was *definitely* the boss!
It made me do it—honest—*cross my heart*!

[verse]
The milk’s not even my favorite kind!
It’s the kind that tastes like cloudy wind!
The cup was *begging* to be dropped!
It was *whispering*, "Let’s get bopped!"

[chorus]
It’s not my fault, it’s not my fault!
The cup was acting like a vault!
It rolled and it tipped and it took a dive—
It’s not my fault it’s still alive!

*Mom* "That’s my kid."
*yawn* I’m too tired to fight…
The milk’s asleep now, the cup’s all right…
Maybe tomorrow… I’ll blame the cat…
*zzz*

[Cover: A cartoon cup with angry eyes and tiny arms mid-leap off a table, milk splashing like a wave, while a kid in socks slides backward in shock, hands up in protest, mouth wide open in a silent "Noooo!"
style_prompt: Catchy children's song, acoustic guitar and beatbox, 117 BPM, strong bass, punchy beat, bouncy groovy infectious singalong, impossible to sit still, makes you move
scene: The kitchen linoleum is sticky under your socks at 5:17 PM, the red plastic cup still rolling after you knocked it off the table—Mom turns from the stove, wooden spoon in hand, and says, *"Who spilled this?"* before the milk even stops spreading.
cover_description: a cartoon child with funny expression in a cozy bedroom
